violations.text: Soup observed thawing in stagnant water. Foods must be thawed under refrigeration, submerged under running water, or in the microwave just prior to cooking. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025
Working container of flour observed not marked with common name of the food. Dry ingredients must be properly labeled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/04/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit (rinse temperature 180 degrees F) was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Establishment must use 3-bay sink to sanitize until dishwasher is serviced and reaching proper sanitizing temperature. New Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2024

ROP salmon observed in the 2-door reach-in cold hold in its original package. ROP foods must be removed from the original package while thawing to ensure food safety. ROP salmon observed to have a temperature of 37 degrees F and was removed from packaging during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/10/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets of the 2-door reach-in were observed in poor repair, splitting away from the doors. Gaskets are to be replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024
